LanguageData.RH_Settings_EnemyHeroRaids_Desc: Now and then a large raid is led by an enemy hero with their own mim retinue. They fall carrying vestment enhancements, and on rare days a class tome.
LanguageData.RH_Settings_AllowHeroCorpseDestruction_D: A fallen hero can still be called back, so by default their corpse cannot be cremated or butchered. Turn this on to lift that protection.
LanguageData.RH_BecameHero: {0} has taken up the path of the {1}.
LanguageData.RH_ScenPart_StartingHeroSummary: Your starting colonist carries a hero's destiny. You will choose their path when the game begins.
LanguageData.RH_DeathsDoorText: {0} has taken wounds that would kill any ordinary soul. Only a hero's destiny holds death at bay.\n\nEach hour, they make a death saving throw. Three successes and they cling to life. Three failures and the thread is cut. Tending their wounds steadies their hand, while any fresh harm counts against them.
LanguageData.RH_DeathsDoorFail: {0} slips closer to death ({1} of {2} failures).
LanguageData.RH_DeathsDoorCritFail: {0} rolls a 1. Death gains two steps on them.
LanguageData.RH_DeathsDoorDamageFail: {0} is struck while dying. The blow counts against them.
LanguageData.RH_DeathsDoorDeath: {0} has lost their last death saving throw.
LanguageData.RH_StabilizedText: {0} has won the fight at death's door and clings to life. The danger has passed, though the wounds remain.
LanguageData.RH_NatTwentyText: Against all odds, {0} rolls a natural 20 on their death saving throw. Their wounds knit shut before the eyes of onlookers. Destiny is not done with them yet.
LanguageData.RH_MimArrived: A {0} mim has come to serve {1}.
LanguageData.RH_MimLost: {1}'s {0} mim has perished. Another will find its way in time.
LanguageData.RH_MimPanic: {0} reels in grief. Its master has fallen.
LanguageData.RH_NoSlots: No level {0} spell slots left. Take a long rest to recover them.
LanguageData.RH_ShortRest: {0} catches their breath, and a spell slot returns (short rest).
LanguageData.RH_LongRestComplete: {0} finishes a long rest. Every spell slot is restored.
LanguageData.RH_LongRestGizmoDesc: Settle in for a long rest, roughly twelve hours of sleep, after which all spell slots return. Progress builds while sleeping. Toggle off to cancel.
LanguageData.RH_AutocastGizmoDesc: Cast {0} on its own when there is a fitting target nearby. Cantrips do this by default; leveled spells must be switched on here.
LanguageData.RH_RevertFormDesc: Shed the beast shape and return to your own.
LanguageData.RH_WildshapeRevertStruck: {0}'s wildshape form is struck down, and they revert to their own shape, badly hurt.
LanguageData.RH_TomeUsedText: {0} has pored over the tome and awakened as a {1}. That destiny is theirs now, and there is no unmaking it.
LanguageData.RH_Revivified: {0} is called back from death by {1}.
LanguageData.RH_TooLongDead: Too long dead. Revivify needs a fresh body.
LanguageData.RH_AlertHeroUnburiedDesc: A hero's body lies unburied. Lay them in a grave or sarcophagus. Calling them back needs a body, and while corpse protection is on, a hero cannot be cremated or butchered.
LanguageData.RH_ChestLooted: You pry open the chest and gather its treasure.
LanguageData.RH_ChestMimic: The chest sprouts teeth. It was a mimic!
LanguageData.RH_CandyNotHero: Only a hero can draw experience from this candy.
LanguageData.RH_CandyMaxLevel: {0} has already reached the height of their path.
LanguageData.RH_CandyEaten: {0} eats the candy and gains {1} experience.
LanguageData.RH_TrickleStart: Monsters are seeping out of the {0}. Delve it or tear it down before it festers.
LanguageData.RH_DungeonSealed: The {0} falls silent. No more monsters will come from it.
LanguageData.RH_EntranceActive: Active: monsters trickle out, faster the longer it stands. Delve and slay its lord, or destroy it, to stop them.
LanguageData.RH_EntranceSealed: Sealed. The flow of monsters has stopped.
LanguageData.RH_QuestSiteText: Word reaches the colony of a {0} out in the wilds, its entrance guarded by the creatures that lair within. Send a caravan to clear the guardians and delve it. The site is marked on your world map.
LanguageData.RH_CapstoneSiteText: The stranger keeps his word. He marks a place on your map: a {0}, deep and deadly, where the promised weapon waits. Delve it and claim {1} from its vault. This is your hero's final trial.
LanguageData.RH_HeroRaidText: A band of hostile adventurers has come for the stranger, and they will cut through your colony to reach him. The party is composed of: {0}.
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryPickDesc: Attempt the reliquary's sigil-lock (DC {0}). Choose who tries. Each failure hardens the lock by 5; let it reach 25 and it jams for good.
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryUseKey: {0} opens it with a reliquary key
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryKeyUsed: {0} sets the reliquary key. Every ward unwinds at once and the lock falls open.
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryNoPicker: No colonist can reach the reliquary
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryOpened: {0} works the lock free. The reliquary gives up what it guarded.
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryFailed: The lock holds and hardens. It will resist the next attempt more (DC {0}).
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryJammed: The lock grinds shut for good. This reliquary will not be opened by craft now.
LanguageData.RH_ReliquaryStateJammed: Jammed: the lock can no longer be picked
LanguageData.RH_TrapDisarmDesc: Carefully neutralize this trap (Perception DC {0}). Choose who tries. Botch it and the trap goes off in their hands.
LanguageData.RH_TrapNoDisarmer: No colonist can reach the trap
LanguageData.RH_TrapDodged: {0} throws themselves clear of the trap.
LanguageData.RH_TrapStateArmed: Armed: step on it and the stepping pawn must dodge, or disarm it by hand
